diff --git a/.chameleon/idioms.md b/.chameleon/idioms.md
index b3b4d74078..1a06304d54 100644
--- a/.chameleon/idioms.md
+++ b/.chameleon/idioms.md
@@ -2,6 +2,34 @@
 
 ## active
 
+### utils-plain-class-no-interaction
+Language: ruby
+Status: active (added 2026-06-15)
+Archetype: service
+Plain utility classes under the Utils:: namespace use only self.* class methods and have no superclass. They are not ActiveInteraction::Base subclasses — no execute method, not run as interactions. This pattern exists alongside ActiveInteraction services in app/services/ and is not a violation.
+
+Example:
+```
+class Utils::RateLimit
+  def self.wait?(limit_name)
+    RedisCache.current.get(rate_limit_key_name(limit_name))
+  end
+
+  def self.wait!(limit_name, expires_seconds)
+    RedisCache.current.set(rate_limit_key_name(limit_name), expires_seconds, ex: expires_seconds)
+  end
+end
+```
+
+Counterexample:
+```
+class Utils::RateLimit < ActiveInteraction::Base
+  def execute
+    # wrong — utils classes are plain Ruby, not interactions
+  end
+end
+```
+
 ### sensitive-attributes-use-has-encrypted
 Language: ruby
 Status: active (added 2026-06-05)
diff --git a/app/services/utils/rate_limit/exponential_backoff.rb b/app/services/utils/rate_limit/exponential_backoff.rb
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000..6d823c0664
--- /dev/null
+++ b/app/services/utils/rate_limit/exponential_backoff.rb
@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@
+# frozen_string_literal: true
+
+class Utils::RateLimit::ExponentialBackoff # chameleon-ignore INHERITANCE
+  def self.wait?(limit_name)
+    RedisCache.current.get(rate_limit_key_name(limit_name))
+  end
+
+  def self.wait!(limit_name, base_seconds:)
+    r = RedisCache.current
+    count = r.incr(tracker_key_name(limit_name))
+    duration = (base_seconds * (2**(count - 1))).to_i
+    r.set(rate_limit_key_name(limit_name), duration, ex: duration)
+  end
+
+  def self.clear!(limit_name)
+    RedisCache.current.del(rate_limit_key_name(limit_name))
+  end
+
+  def self.reset!(limit_name)
+    r = RedisCache.current
+    r.del(rate_limit_key_name(limit_name))
+    r.del(tracker_key_name(limit_name))
+  end
+
+  def self.hit_count(limit_name)
+    RedisCache.current.get(tracker_key_name(limit_name)).to_i
+  end
+
+  def self.rate_limit_key_name(limit_name)
+    Digest::SHA1.hexdigest(limit_name).prepend('rate-limit:backoff:')
+  end
+
+  def self.tracker_key_name(limit_name)
+    Digest::SHA1.hexdigest(limit_name).prepend('tracker:backoff:')
+  end
+end
diff --git a/spec/services/utils/rate_limit/exponential_backoff_spec.rb b/spec/services/utils/rate_limit/exponential_backoff_spec.rb
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000..250fa1985d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/spec/services/utils/rate_limit/exponential_backoff_spec.rb
@@ -0,0 +1,90 @@
+# frozen_string_literal: true
+
+require 'rails_helper'
+
+RSpec.describe Utils::RateLimit::ExponentialBackoff, type: :service do
+  let(:limit_name) { 'test-exponential-backoff' }
+  let(:base_seconds) { 60 }
+
+  before { described_class.reset!(limit_name) }
+
+  describe '.wait?' do
+    it 'returns nil when no backoff is active' do
+      expect(described_class.wait?(limit_name)).to be_nil
+    end
+
+    it 'returns the duration when a backoff is active' do
+      described_class.wait!(limit_name, base_seconds: base_seconds)
+      expect(described_class.wait?(limit_name).to_i).to eq(base_seconds)
+    end
+  end
+
+  describe '.wait!' do
+    it 'sets the backoff flag in Redis' do
+      described_class.wait!(limit_name, base_seconds: base_seconds)
+      expect(described_class.wait?(limit_name)).to be_present
+    end
+
+    it 'doubles the wait duration on each subsequent hit' do
+      described_class.wait!(limit_name, base_seconds: base_seconds)
+      described_class.clear!(limit_name)
+      described_class.wait!(limit_name, base_seconds: base_seconds)
+      expect(described_class.wait?(limit_name).to_i).to eq(base_seconds * 2)
+    end
+
+    it 'grows exponentially across three hits' do
+      described_class.wait!(limit_name, base_seconds: base_seconds)
+      described_class.clear!(limit_name)
+      described_class.wait!(limit_name, base_seconds: base_seconds)
+      described_class.clear!(limit_name)
+      described_class.wait!(limit_name, base_seconds: base_seconds)
+      expect(described_class.wait?(limit_name).to_i).to eq(base_seconds * 4)
+    end
+
+    it 'increments the hit counter on each call' do
+      3.times { described_class.wait!(limit_name, base_seconds: base_seconds) }
+      expect(described_class.hit_count(limit_name)).to eq(3)
+    end
+  end
+
+  describe '.clear!' do
+    it 'removes the backoff flag' do
+      described_class.wait!(limit_name, base_seconds: base_seconds)
+      described_class.clear!(limit_name)
+      expect(described_class.wait?(limit_name)).to be_nil
+    end
+
+    it 'preserves the hit counter so subsequent waits remain exponential' do
+      described_class.wait!(limit_name, base_seconds: base_seconds)
+      described_class.clear!(limit_name)
+      expect(described_class.hit_count(limit_name)).to eq(1)
+    end
+  end
+
+  describe '.hit_count' do
+    it 'returns 0 when the rate limit has never been triggered' do
+      expect(described_class.hit_count(limit_name)).to eq(0)
+    end
+
+    it 'returns the number of times wait! has been called' do
+      3.times { described_class.wait!(limit_name, base_seconds: base_seconds) }
+      expect(described_class.hit_count(limit_name)).to eq(3)
+    end
+  end
+
+  describe '.reset!' do
+    it 'clears both the backoff flag and the hit counter' do
+      3.times { described_class.wait!(limit_name, base_seconds: base_seconds) }
+      described_class.reset!(limit_name)
+      expect(described_class.wait?(limit_name)).to be_nil
+      expect(described_class.hit_count(limit_name)).to eq(0)
+    end
+
+    it 'causes the next wait! to use base_seconds as if hitting for the first time' do
+      3.times { described_class.wait!(limit_name, base_seconds: base_seconds) }
+      described_class.reset!(limit_name)
+      described_class.wait!(limit_name, base_seconds: base_seconds)
+      expect(described_class.wait?(limit_name).to_i).to eq(base_seconds)
+    end
+  end
+end

+import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest"
+
+import { generateFormLabel } from "../strings"
+
+describe("generateFormLabel", () => {
+  it("converts snake_case column names to title case", () => {
+    expect(generateFormLabel("agreed_upon_commission")).toBe("Agreed Upon Commission")
+  })
+
+  it("handles single word columns", () => {
+    expect(generateFormLabel("price")).toBe("Price")
+  })
+
+  it("handles multiple underscores", () => {
+    expect(generateFormLabel("first_name_last_name")).toBe("First Name Last Name")
+  })
+
+  it("returns empty string for empty input", () => {
+    expect(generateFormLabel("")).toBe("")
+  })
+})

+  describe.each([
+    [null, null],
+    [undefined, null],
+    ["", null],
+    ["$", null],
+    ["abc", null],
+    ["$0", 0],
+    ["$1", 1],
+    ["$ 0.00", 0],
+    ["$ 1.23", 1.23],
+    ["$ 1,234.56", 1234.56],
+    ["$ 1,234,567.89", 1234567.89],
+    ["$1000", 1000],
+    ["1234", 1234],
+    ["1234.56", 1234.56],
+  ])("parseMoneyValue(%s)", (input, expected) => {
+    it(`returns ${expected}`, () => {
+      expect(parseMoneyValue(input)).toBe(expected)
+    })
+  })
+})
diff --git a/src/components/base/MoneyField/index.tsx b/src/components/base/MoneyField/index.tsx
new file mode 100644
index 000000000..accd69154
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/components/base/MoneyField/index.tsx
@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@
+import * as React from "react"
+
+import { FieldFormik, TFieldFormik } from "~/components/base/Field/FieldFormik"
+
+export type TMoneyFieldProps = Omit<TFieldFormik, "maskName" | "type">
+
+export const MoneyField: React.FC<TMoneyFieldProps> = ({ maxLength = 20, ...props }) => (
+  <FieldFormik maskName="usdWithDecimal" maxLength={maxLength} {...props} />
+)
+
+export const parseMoneyValue = (value: string | null | undefined): number | null => {
+  if (!value) return null
+  const stripped = value.replace(/[^0-9.-]+/g, "")
+  if (!stripped || stripped === "-") return null
+  const parsed = Number(stripped)
+  if (isNaN(parsed)) return null
+  return parsed
+}

+import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest"
+
+import { getScrollDirection } from "~/utils/scrollDirection"
+
+describe("getScrollDirection", () => {
+  it("returns 0 when there is no previous position", () => {
+    expect(getScrollDirection(100, undefined)).toBe(0)
+  })
+
+  it("returns 1 when scrolling down", () => {
+    expect(getScrollDirection(200, 100)).toBe(1)
+  })
+
+  it("returns -1 when scrolling up", () => {
+    expect(getScrollDirection(50, 200)).toBe(-1)
+  })
+
+  it("returns 0 when position has not changed", () => {
+    expect(getScrollDirection(100, 100)).toBe(0)
+  })
+})
diff --git a/src/hooks/useScrollDirection.ts b/src/hooks/useScrollDirection.ts
new file mode 100644
index 000000000..0e57c1eab
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/hooks/useScrollDirection.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,10 @@
+import { usePrevious } from "~/hooks/usePrevious"
+import { useWindowScroll } from "~/hooks/useWindowScroll"
+import { getScrollDirection } from "~/utils/scrollDirection"
+
+export const useScrollDirection = (): -1 | 0 | 1 => {
+  const { scrollY } = useWindowScroll()
+  const current = scrollY ?? 0
+  const previous = usePrevious(current)
+  return getScrollDirection(current, previous)
+}
diff --git a/src/utils/scrollDirection.ts b/src/utils/scrollDirection.ts
new file mode 100644
index 000000000..d131075a7
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/utils/scrollDirection.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,4 @@
+export const getScrollDirection = (current: number, previous: number | undefined): -1 | 0 | 1 => {
+  if (previous === undefined) return 0
+  return Math.sign(current - previous) as -1 | 0 | 1
+}

diff --git a/src/hooks/useScrollDirection.ts b/src/hooks/useScrollDirection.ts
new file mode 100644
index 000000000..90048cc66
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/hooks/useScrollDirection.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+import { usePrevious } from "~/hooks/usePrevious"
+import { useWindowScroll } from "~/hooks/useWindowScroll"
+
+export const useScrollDirection = (): -1 | 0 | 1 => {
+  const { scrollY } = useWindowScroll()
+  const previousScrollY = usePrevious(scrollY)
+
+  if (scrollY === null || previousScrollY == null || scrollY === previousScrollY) return 0
+
+  return scrollY > previousScrollY ? 1 : -1
+}

diff --git a/src/hooks/useScrollDirection.test.ts b/src/hooks/useScrollDirection.test.ts
new file mode 100644
index 000000000..4ae792a17
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/hooks/useScrollDirection.test.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,60 @@
+import * as React from "react"
+import { createRoot } from "react-dom/client"
+import { act } from "react"
+import { describe, it, expect, beforeEach, afterEach } from "vitest"
+
+import { useScrollDirection } from "./useScrollDirection"
+
+describe("useScrollDirection", () => {
+  let container: HTMLDivElement
+  let direction: -1 | 0 | 1
+  let root: ReturnType<typeof createRoot>
+
+  const TestComponent = () => {
+    direction = useScrollDirection()
+    return null
+  }
+
+  const scroll = (y: number) => {
+    Object.defineProperty(window, "pageYOffset", { value: y, writable: true, configurable: true })
+    act(() => {
+      window.dispatchEvent(new Event("scroll"))
+    })
+  }
+
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    Object.defineProperty(window, "pageYOffset", { value: 0, writable: true, configurable: true })
+    container = document.createElement("div")
+    document.body.appendChild(container)
+    root = createRoot(container)
+    act(() => {
+      root.render(React.createElement(TestComponent))
+    })
+  })
+
+  afterEach(() => {
+    act(() => root.unmount())
+    document.body.removeChild(container)
+  })
+
+  it("returns 0 initially", () => {
+    expect(direction).toBe(0)
+  })
+
+  it("returns 1 when scrolling down", () => {
+    scroll(200)
+    expect(direction).toBe(1)
+  })
+
+  it("returns -1 when scrolling up", () => {
+    scroll(200)
+    scroll(50)
+    expect(direction).toBe(-1)
+  })
+
+  it("returns 0 when position is unchanged", () => {
+    scroll(100)
+    scroll(100)
+    expect(direction).toBe(0)
+  })
+})
diff --git a/src/hooks/useScrollDirection.ts b/src/hooks/useScrollDirection.ts
new file mode 100644
index 000000000..5a9cf8038
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/hooks/useScrollDirection.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+import * as React from "react"
+
+import { usePrevious } from "~/hooks/usePrevious"
+
+export const useScrollDirection = (): -1 | 0 | 1 => {
+  const [scrollY, setScrollY] = React.useState(window.pageYOffset)
+  const previousScrollY = usePrevious(scrollY)
+
+  React.useEffect(() => {
+    const handleScroll = () => setScrollY(window.pageYOffset)
+    window.addEventListener("scroll", handleScroll)
+    return () => window.removeEventListener("scroll", handleScroll)
+  }, [])
+
+  if (previousScrollY === undefined) return 0
+  return Math.sign(scrollY - previousScrollY) as -1 | 0 | 1
+}

+
+  describe("notBlankString", () => {
+    it("rejects empty string", async () => {
+      await expect(notBlankString().validate("")).rejects.toThrow()
+    })
+
+    it("rejects whitespace-only string", async () => {
+      await expect(notBlankString().validate("   ")).rejects.toThrow()
+    })
+
+    it("rejects tab-only string", async () => {
+      await expect(notBlankString().validate("\t")).rejects.toThrow()
+    })
+
+    it("rejects newline-only string", async () => {
+      await expect(notBlankString().validate("\n")).rejects.toThrow()
+    })
+
+    it("rejects mixed whitespace", async () => {
+      await expect(notBlankString().validate("  \t\n  ")).rejects.toThrow()
+    })
+
+    it("accepts a non-blank string", async () => {
+      await expect(notBlankString().validate("hello")).resolves.toBe("hello")
+    })
+
+    it("accepts a string with surrounding whitespace but real content", async () => {
+      await expect(notBlankString().validate("  hello  ")).resolves.toBe("  hello  ")
+    })
+
+    it("passes null through so required() can handle it", async () => {
+      await expect(notBlankString().nullable().validate(null)).resolves.toBeNull()
+    })
+
+    it("passes undefined through so required() can handle it", async () => {
+      await expect(notBlankString().optional().validate(undefined)).resolves.toBeUndefined()
+    })
+
+    it("uses a custom error message", async () => {
+      await expect(notBlankString("Cannot be blank").validate("   ")).rejects.toThrow(
+        "Cannot be blank"
+      )
+    })
+  })
 })

diff --git a/src/utils/__tests__/localStorageExpiry.test.ts b/src/utils/__tests__/localStorageExpiry.test.ts
new file mode 100644
index 000000000..9bc1a3166
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/utils/__tests__/localStorageExpiry.test.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,85 @@
+import { afterEach, beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from "vitest"
+
+import {
+  getItemWithExpiry,
+  getSessionItemWithExpiry,
+  setItemWithExpiry,
+  setSessionItemWithExpiry,
+} from "../localStorageExpiry"
+
+describe("setItemWithExpiry / getItemWithExpiry", () => {
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    vi.useFakeTimers()
+    localStorage.clear()
+  })
+
+  afterEach(() => {
+    vi.useRealTimers()
+  })
+
+  it("returns stored data before expiry", () => {
+    setItemWithExpiry("key", "value", 60)
+    expect(getItemWithExpiry("key")).toBe("value")
+  })
+
+  it("returns null for a missing key", () => {
+    expect(getItemWithExpiry("missing")).toBeNull()
+  })
+
+  it("returns null and removes the item after expiry", () => {
+    setItemWithExpiry("key", "value", 60)
+    vi.advanceTimersByTime(61_000)
+    expect(getItemWithExpiry("key")).toBeNull()
+    expect(localStorage.getItem("key")).toBeNull()
+  })
+
+  it("still returns data one second before expiry", () => {
+    setItemWithExpiry("key", "value", 60)
+    vi.advanceTimersByTime(59_000)
+    expect(getItemWithExpiry("key")).toBe("value")
+  })
+})
+
+describe("setSessionItemWithExpiry / getSessionItemWithExpiry", () => {
+  beforeEach(() => {
+    vi.useFakeTimers()
+    sessionStorage.clear()
+    localStorage.clear()
+  })
+
+  afterEach(() => {
+    vi.useRealTimers()
+  })
+
+  it("returns stored data before expiry", () => {
+    setSessionItemWithExpiry("key", "value", 60)
+    expect(getSessionItemWithExpiry("key")).toBe("value")
+  })
+
+  it("returns null for a missing key", () => {
+    expect(getSessionItemWithExpiry("missing")).toBeNull()
+  })
+
+  it("returns null and removes the item after expiry", () => {
+    setSessionItemWithExpiry("key", "value", 60)
+    vi.advanceTimersByTime(61_000)
+    expect(getSessionItemWithExpiry("key")).toBeNull()
+    expect(sessionStorage.getItem("key")).toBeNull()
+  })
+
+  it("still returns data one second before expiry", () => {
+    setSessionItemWithExpiry("key", "value", 60)
+    vi.advanceTimersByTime(59_000)
+    expect(getSessionItemWithExpiry("key")).toBe("value")
+  })
+
+  it("reads from sessionStorage, not localStorage", () => {
+    setItemWithExpiry("key", "local-value", 60)
+    expect(getSessionItemWithExpiry("key")).toBeNull()
+  })
+
+  it("writes to sessionStorage, not localStorage", () => {
+    setSessionItemWithExpiry("key", "value", 60)
+    expect(localStorage.getItem("key")).toBeNull()
+  })
+})
diff --git a/src/utils/localStorageExpiry.ts b/src/utils/localStorageExpiry.ts
index 54f14a272..95ed5a8d6 100644
--- a/src/utils/localStorageExpiry.ts
+++ b/src/utils/localStorageExpiry.ts
@@ -1,29 +1,32 @@
-// Use this when you want to set a localStorage item that should expire after
-// x amount of seconds
-export const setItemWithExpiry = (
-  key: string,
-  data: string,
-  expiryInSeconds: number
-) => {
+type StoredItem = { expiry: string; data: string }
+
+const setWithExpiry = (storage: Storage, key: string, data: string, expiryInSeconds: number): void => {
   const expiry = new Date()
   expiry.setSeconds(expiry.getSeconds() + expiryInSeconds)
-
-  const item = { expiry, data }
-  localStorage.setItem(key, JSON.stringify(item))
+  storage.setItem(key, JSON.stringify({ expiry, data }))
 }
 
-// Use this to retrieve an item that should expire. If expired, this will
-// return null
-export const getItemWithExpiry = (key: string) => {
-  const item = localStorage.getItem(key)
+const getWithExpiry = (storage: Storage, key: string): string | null => {
+  const item = storage.getItem(key)
   if (!item) return null
 
-  const itemParsed: { expiry: string; data: string } = JSON.parse(item)
-  if (new Date() > new Date(itemParsed.expiry)) {
-    // If item is expired, delete it and return null
-    localStorage.removeItem(key)
+  const parsed: StoredItem = JSON.parse(item)
+  if (new Date() > new Date(parsed.expiry)) {
+    storage.removeItem(key)
     return null
   }
 
-  return itemParsed.data
+  return parsed.data
 }
+
+export const setItemWithExpiry = (key: string, data: string, expiryInSeconds: number): void =>
+  setWithExpiry(localStorage, key, data, expiryInSeconds)
+
+export const getItemWithExpiry = (key: string): string | null =>
+  getWithExpiry(localStorage, key)
+
+export const setSessionItemWithExpiry = (key: string, data: string, expiryInSeconds: number): void =>
+  setWithExpiry(sessionStorage, key, data, expiryInSeconds)
+
+export const getSessionItemWithExpiry = (key: string): string | null =>
+  getWithExpiry(sessionStorage, key)

diff --git a/src/utils/__tests__/user.test.ts b/src/utils/__tests__/user.test.ts
new file mode 100644
index 000000000..1897ee608
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/utils/__tests__/user.test.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,31 @@
+import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest"
+
+import { getFullName } from "../user"
+
+describe("getFullName", () => {
+  it("returns first and last name when no preferred name is set", () => {
+    expect(getFullName({ firstName: "Alice", lastName: "Smith" })).toBe("Alice Smith")
+  })
+
+  it("uses preferred name in place of first name when available", () => {
+    expect(
+      getFullName({ firstName: "Alice", lastName: "Smith", preferredName: "Ali" })
+    ).toBe("Ali Smith")
+  })
+
+  it("returns empty string when first name is missing", () => {
+    expect(getFullName({ lastName: "Smith" })).toBe("")
+  })
+
+  it("returns empty string when last name is missing", () => {
+    expect(getFullName({ firstName: "Alice" })).toBe("")
+  })
+
+  it("returns empty string when user is null", () => {
+    expect(getFullName(null)).toBe("")
+  })
+
+  it("returns empty string when user is undefined", () => {
+    expect(getFullName(undefined)).toBe("")
+  })
+})

diff --git a/src/components/ListingDetail/ListingUrlDisplay.tsx b/src/components/ListingDetail/ListingUrlDisplay.tsx
new file mode 100644
index 000000000..e81b75727
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/components/ListingDetail/ListingUrlDisplay.tsx
@@ -0,0 +1,40 @@
+import * as React from "react"
+
+import styled from "styled-components"
+
+import { Link } from "~/components/base"
+import { IListingShowSites } from "~/types/Listing"
+import { removeHttpPrefix } from "~/utils/url"
+
+interface IProps {
+  sites: IListingShowSites[]
+}
+
+export const ListingUrlDisplay: React.FC<IProps> = ({ sites }) => {
+  if (!sites?.length) {
+    return null
+  }
+
+  return (
+    <Wrapper>
+      {sites.map((site) => (
+        <Link key={site.id} to={site.url} plain target="_blank">
+          <UrlText>{removeHttpPrefix(site.url)}</UrlText>
+        </Link>
+      ))}
+    </Wrapper>
+  )
+}
+
+const Wrapper = styled.div`
+  display: flex;
+  flex-direction: column;
+  align-items: flex-start;
+  gap: 0.5rem;
+`
+
+const UrlText = styled.span`
+  overflow: hidden;
+  text-overflow: ellipsis;
+  white-space: nowrap;
+`
diff --git a/src/utils/__tests__/url.test.ts b/src/utils/__tests__/url.test.ts
new file mode 100644
index 000000000..68606fb6e
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/utils/__tests__/url.test.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,27 @@
+import { describe, expect, it } from "vitest"
+
+import { removeHttpPrefix } from "../url"
+
+describe("removeHttpPrefix", () => {
+  it("strips https:// prefix", () => {
+    expect(removeHttpPrefix("https://www.example.com")).toBe("www.example.com")
+  })
+
+  it("strips http:// prefix", () => {
+    expect(removeHttpPrefix("http://www.example.com")).toBe("www.example.com")
+  })
+
+  it("preserves the path and query string", () => {
+    expect(removeHttpPrefix("https://example.com/path?q=1")).toBe(
+      "example.com/path?q=1"
+    )
+  })
+
+  it("returns undefined for undefined input", () => {
+    expect(removeHttpPrefix(undefined)).toBeUndefined()
+  })
+
+  it("leaves a URL with no protocol unchanged", () => {
+    expect(removeHttpPrefix("www.example.com")).toBe("www.example.com")
+  })
+})
